title: dianthus sylvestris subsp. garganicus
intro: Dianthus sylvestris subsp. garganicus, also known as the Garganic pink or wild pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and providing antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy. The herbal preparations of Dianthus sylvestris subsp. garganicus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
